summary: Simon reflects on a personal experience from his apprenticeship where he learned the value of sharing knowledge freely. Initially reluctant to share exam summaries he and a friend created, he eventually opened up and discovered that sharing led to receiving more input, better relationships, and valuable discussions. He connects this lesson to the rise of open source software and encourages readers to embrace a sharing mindset.
key_points: Initial reluctance to share hard work is natural but counterproductive
Sharing openly leads to receiving more valuable input in return
The act of sharing builds appreciation, relationships, and friendships
Discussions arising from sharing generate new ideas and insights
Open source's popularity validates the power of collaborative sharing
money_quotes: The more you share the more you get
After I took that step, I received a lot more input for our summaries. Everyone knew we were working on them, so everyone sent us helpful information and content for the document.
People will appreciate that you share something they can use without asking for something in return. AND, later on, they will remember you when they find something that might be of interest to you!
Maybe even more valuable than this, are the discussions that arise from sharing, which will inevitably lead to many new ideas.
It's no surprise that open source software or open source, in general, is more popular than ever before as it is all about sharing and learning from each other.
key_insight: Freely sharing knowledge creates a virtuous cycle where generosity is reciprocated with more input, stronger relationships, and collaborative innovation.
